title: Operating status identification system for machine
abstract: An operating status identification system (30) for a machine (10) includes a position sensing module (32) configured to generate a first signal value corresponding to a speed of the machine (10). The operating status identification system (30) also includes a motion sensing module (34) configured to generate a second signal value corresponding to at least one motion parameter of the machine (10). The operating status identification system (30) further includes a control module (36) communicably coupled to each of the position sensing module (32) and the motion sensing module (34). The control module (36) is configured to receive the first signal value and the second signal value. The control module (36) is further configured to identify whether the machine (10) is in at least one of an operating state and a non-operating state based on a combination of the first and second signal values. The control module (36) is configured to identify that the machine (10) is in the operating state when the machine (10) is stationary.
claims: Claims Claims 1. An operating status identification system (30) for a machine (10) comprises: a position sensing module (32) mounted on the machine (10), wherein the position sensing module (32) is configured to generate a first signal value corresponding to a speed of the machine (10); a motion sensing module (34) mounted on the machine (10), wherein the motion sensing module (34) is configured to generate a second signal value corresponding to at least one motion parameter of the machine (10); and a control module (36) communicably coupled to each of the position sensing module (32) and the motion sensing module (34), wherein the control module (36) is configured to: receive the first signal value corresponding to the speed of the machine (10) from the position sensing module (32); receive the second signal value corresponding to the at least one motion parameter of the machine (10) from the motion sensing module (34); and identify whether the machine (10) is in at least one of an operating state and a non-operating state based on a combination of the first and second signal values received by the control module (36), wherein the control module (36) is configured to identify that the machine (10) is in the operating state when the machine (10) is stationary.
